name: Pillars Fund
mission: Pillars Fund amplifies the leadership, narratives, and talents of Muslims in the United States to advance opportunity and justice for all.
theoryOfChange: Tapping into Muslim storytelling to transform society and challenge stereotypes through authentic representation in film and media.
scoreSummary: Pillars Fund shows good impact and moderate alignment.

evaluationSources: Research published in partnership with Annenberg Inclusion Initiative and Ford Foundation

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: CEO compensation is 3.1% of revenue ($185,000 on $5,966,431)
keyConcerns.detail: For orgs with revenue $5-50M, CEO pay above 2% of revenue warrants scrutiny.

components.evidence: Muslim-focused organization (+2); Islamic identity (+1); Asnaf: fi_sabilillah (+5)
components.improvement_suggestion: Add a dedicated zakat page or clearly state whether donations are zakat-eligible.
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

alignment.rationale: Alignment 25/50: MEDIUM Muslim donor fit, religious cultural cause area

components.evidence: No beneficiary data available for cost-per-beneficiary scoring; moderate program ratio (≥65%)
components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.
components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

components.improvement_suggestion: If reserves are above policy target, document why and publish triggers for planned deployment.
components.name: Financial Health

components.improvement_suggestion: Document a clear theory of change with an explicit causal pathway.
components.name: Theory of Change

components.evidence: Board: 9 members, but For orgs with revenue $5-50M, CEO pay above 2% of revenue warrants scrutiny. — oversight concern
components.name: Governance

judge_issues.message: Expenses ($4,783,807) exceed revenue ($2,398,957) by 99% - may be drawing down reserves

risks.description: CEO compensation is 3.1% of revenue ($185,000 on $5,966,431)

score_details.score_summary: Pillars Fund shows good impact and moderate alignment.

zakat.claim_evidence: Pillars Fund is a grant-making organization and Muslim community foundation named in reference to the five pillars of Islam, the third of which is Zakat. Pillars is focused on funding secular non-profit programs supporting American Muslims in media, public relations, and leadership development. (Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pillars_Fund)

all_citations.claim: The organization maintains a program expense ratio of 74.3%.
Total revenue for the period was $5,966,431.
Pillars Fund strategically supports nonprofit organizations that amplify the talents of American Muslims.
The organization invests and leverages funds to support its external affairs and program goals.
The organization produces multimedia publications to support its storytelling mission.
Pillars Fund utilizes multimedia publications as a core metric for its storytelling programs.
The organization provides various methods for general charitable giving and sadaqah.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: Pillars Fund demonstrates solid financial health and a clear niche in cultural advocacy, though its overall score is moderated by its specialized focus on capacity building rather than direct service.
areas_for_improvement: Limited third-party impact data regarding long-term social outcomes
Moderate alignment for donors specifically seeking high-urgency humanitarian relief

dimension_explanations.alignment: Pillars Fund offers a medium fit for donors interested in religious and cultural advocacy, providing a clear pathway for sadaqah-eligible contributions [8]. The organization has a total revenue of $5,966,431, reflecting a stable track record in its specific sector [2].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization maintains a program expense ratio of 74.3% and focuses on capacity building through multimedia publications and artist fellowships [1][2][7]. While it effectively leverages funds for storytelling, the direct impact on beneficiaries is categorized under long-term cultural shifts rather than immediate relief [4].

baseline_narrative.headline: Pillars Fund is a philanthropic organization dedicated to amplifying the talents of American Muslims through strategic grantmaking and storytelling initiatives.
strengths: Strong financial stability with 20.7 months of working capital
High fundraising efficiency, spending only $0.12 to raise every $1
Dedicated focus on capacity building within the American Muslim creative community
baseline_narrative.summary: Pillars Fund strategically supports nonprofit organizations and individual artists to strengthen relations between American Muslims and the broader public [3]. Through its Storytelling Grants and the Pillars Artist Fellowship, the organization invests in cultural narratives that challenge stereotypes and showcase Muslim contributions to society [4][5].

all_citations.quote: To strengthen relations with American Muslims and all Americans.

all_citations.source_name: Pillars Fund - VP External Affairs JD

all_citations.claim: Bronze Seal of Transparency from Candid

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: Pillars Fund demonstrates solid financial health and a clear niche in cultural advocacy, though its overall score is moderated by its specialized focus on capacity building rather than direct service.

areas_for_improvement.context: The organization has experienced a <cite id="15">3-year revenue CAGR of -53.4%</cite>, suggesting a need for more diversified or sustainable long-term funding streams.
The charity currently holds a <cite id="16">Bronze Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, indicating room to share more detailed outcome data.

bbb_assessment.summary: BBB assessment data is currently unavailable for this organization.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization's high working capital of 19 months provides a significant buffer against recent revenue drops.
risk_factors: <cite id="15">Negative 53.4% revenue growth over three years</cite>
<cite id="22">CEO compensation above 2% of revenue</cite> may warrant donor scrutiny
case_against.summary: The primary concerns involve <cite id="15">declining revenue trends</cite> and a <cite id="21">lack of third-party impact evaluations</cite> for its storytelling initiatives.

confidence.notes: Financial data is robust, but impact measurement is qualitative.

alignment.explanation: The fund is a strong fit for donors interested in <cite id="1">American Muslim storytelling</cite> and <cite id="19">capacity building within the community</cite>.
alignment.improvement: Donors would benefit if the organization <cite id="20">clearly stated its policy on sadaqah-only contributions</cite> to avoid confusion regarding other forms of giving.

credibility.explanation: Pillars Fund demonstrates high credibility through an <cite id="14">88% independent board</cite> and a <cite id="16">Bronze Seal from Candid</cite>. They maintain <cite id="17">audited financial statements</cite> to ensure fiscal accountability.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the charity could <cite id="16">pursue a higher Candid seal</cite> by publishing more comprehensive strategy and outcome documents.

impact.explanation: Impact is achieved through <cite id="11">extensive domestic grantmaking</cite> and a <cite id="9">74.3% program ratio</cite>, focusing on long-term cultural shifts.
impact.improvement: The organization should <cite id="18">publish consistent beneficiary counts</cite> to allow for better assessment of cost-effectiveness across its fellowship programs.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade C - Verified outcomes with developing theory of change

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Strategic investments in nonprofit organizations and individual artists to amplify Muslim talent.

rich_narrative.headline: Pillars Fund is a philanthropic organization dedicated to amplifying the talents of American Muslims through strategic grantmaking and storytelling initiatives.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to invest in the long-term cultural and creative infrastructure of the American Muslim community through strategic grantmaking.
donor_motivations: Value shifting public narratives about Muslims
Prefer supporting a broad ecosystem of grassroots nonprofits
Interested in the intersection of faith, art, and civic engagement
Want to support domestic capacity building
giving_considerations: Impact is measured in cultural shifts rather than immediate humanitarian relief
The organization acts as an intermediary funder
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fulfill zakat obligations or those looking for immediate, high-urgency humanitarian aid like food or medicine.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects verified program delivery and outcome tracking, though independent social impact evaluations are not yet public.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: Pillars Fund invests in Muslim creators and leaders to challenge harmful stereotypes and build a more just society through narrative change.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence in storytelling helps prove that cultural investments lead to measurable changes in public perception.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: Significant revenue declines suggest a high need for new donor support to maintain current grantmaking levels.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="4">exclusive focus on storytelling and artist fellowships</cite> rather than direct social services.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Direct service provider and top grant recipient
similar_organizations.name: Inner City Muslim Action Network

similar_organizations.differentiator: Focuses specifically on academic and media scholarships
similar_organizations.name: Islamic Scholarship Fund

strengths.detail: The organization maintains <cite id="8">20.7 months of working capital</cite>, providing a significant buffer for long-term project commitments.
Pillars Fund directs a <cite id="9">74.3% program expense ratio</cite> toward its mission-driven activities and grantmaking.
The charity demonstrates lean operations, spending only <cite id="10">$0.31 to raise every $1</cite> in contribution revenue.

strengths_deep_dive: Pillars Fund excels in its role as a grantmaker, distributing <cite id="11">$6,560,000 in total grants</cite> during the most recent reporting period. This includes substantial support for civic rights, evidenced by a <cite id="12">$135,000 grant to Defending Rights and Dissent</cite>. Their commitment to cultural production is further highlighted by the creation of <cite id="13">multimedia publications</cite> that document Muslim experiences. This high-impact grantmaking is supported by a <cite id="14">board where 88% of members are independent</cite>, ensuring strong oversight of the fund's strategic direction.
rich_narrative.summary: Pillars Fund operates as a strategic philanthropic hub dedicated to <cite id="1">amplifying the talents of American Muslims</cite> through targeted grantmaking and cultural storytelling. Unlike traditional humanitarian charities, Pillars focuses on <cite id="2">strengthening relations with American Muslims</cite> by investing in the creative and civic infrastructure of the community. The organization reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $2,398,957 in 2023</cite>, reflecting its role as a specialized funder in the religious and cultural space. Their work is primarily delivered through <cite id="4">Storytelling Grants, the Pillars Artist Fellowship, and Media Labs</cite>, which aim to shift public narratives. Since its inception, the fund has <cite id="5">invested and leveraged over $30 million</cite> to support Muslim leaders and creators. By providing <cite id="6">domestic grants to 111 recipients</cite>, Pillars Fund acts as a critical intermediary, directing resources to grassroots organizations like the <cite id="7">Inner City Muslim Action Network</cite> and Detroit Revival Engaging Muslims. This model of capacity building ensures that donor contributions support a wide ecosystem of Muslim-led initiatives rather than a single program.
